title: commiphora agallocha
intro: Commiphora agallocha, also known as Indian myrrh or guggul, is a plant species in the family Burseraceae, native to the Indian subcontinent and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including powder, oil, and resin, which are used in traditional medicine and as a natural remedy for various health conditions. The resin of Commiphora agallocha, in particular, is often used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties and is also used as a fragrance and incense ingredient.
